files.year_nibbles_format: uint8_t[30000001]; nibble[orderkey] = (year-1992) in lower nibble of byte[orderkey/2] for even orderkeys, upper nibble for odd; 0xF = no order

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_nibbles.bin with MADV_WILLNEED to pre-warm 28.6MB into LLC. On hot path: uint32_t lorderkey = (uint32_t)l_orderkey[i]; int year_off = (nibbles[lorderkey >> 1] >> ((lorderkey & 1) << 2)) & 0xF; — replaces the 2-DRAM-miss serial chain (orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey] -> o_orderdate[orow] -> extract_year) with a single L3-resident byte access. Do NOT mmap or read orders_by_orderkey.bin or o_orderdate.bin.

join_order.notes: Build uint8_t green_part[2000001] indexed by p_partkey. ~5.5% selectivity yields ~3.3M surviving lineitem rows.
Precompute int32_t suppkey_to_nationrow[100001] at startup: load supplier_by_suppkey.bin + s_nationkey.bin + nation_by_nationkey.bin, fold two-level indirection into one array. 0.4MB, L3-resident.
Use year_nibbles.bin (28.6MB mmap, MADV_WILLNEED). year_off = (nibbles[lorderkey>>1] >> ((lorderkey&1)<<2)) & 0xF. L3-resident. Do NOT open orders_by_orderkey.bin or o_orderdate.bin.

Use pre-built partsupp_hash_index.bin (256MB mmap, MADV_RANDOM). Composite key = partkey*200001ULL + suppkey, Fibonacci hash. Issue __builtin_prefetch for hash slot PREFETCH_DIST=16 iterations ahead on qualifying rows to hide the DRAM miss latency.

data_structures.green_part_filter: uint8_t flat array[2000001], indexed by p_partkey — 2MB, L3-resident
data_structures.suppkey_to_nationrow: int32_t flat array[100001], precomputed at startup — 0.4MB, L3-resident
data_structures.year_nibbles: uint8_t mmap array[30000001] from year_nibbles.bin — 28.6MB, MADV_WILLNEED, L3-resident
data_structures.partsupp_lookup: mmap partsupp_hash_index HTSlot[16777216] (256MB) + ps_supplycost double[] (61MB), MADV_RANDOM
data_structures.aggregation: double profits[25][10] per thread, cache-line aligned (alignas(64) struct), zero-initialized

parallelism.thread_local_state: double profits[25][10] per thread (NOT long double — must be 64-bit IEEE double)
parallelism.reduction: single-threaded sum of per-thread profits[25][10] after parallel phase

indexes.purpose: O(1) filter of lineitem rows by l_partkey
O(1) l_suppkey -> nation row, single-level precomputed
O(1) l_orderkey -> year_offset (0-6) from 28.6MB L3-resident array; replaces 2-miss serial orders chain
O(1) avg (l_partkey, l_suppkey) -> ps_supplycost row; 256MB, DRAM-exceeding, use software prefetch to hide miss latency

optimization_notes: Root cause (Q2): the hot path's orders lookup is a 2-DRAM-miss serial dependency chain — orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey] (240MB, DRAM miss) then o_orderdate[orow] (57MB, DRAM miss dependent on first) — totaling ~200ns per qualifying row. The pre-built year_nibbles.bin (28.6MB) already exists and collapses this chain to a single L3-resident nibble access (~10ns). Strategy: Category E (use_existing) — mmap year_nibbles.bin with MADV_WILLNEED to pre-warm into LLC before the scan starts; on the hot path use the nibble formula instead of the orders two-level lookup; eliminate all opens of orders_by_orderkey.bin and o_orderdate.bin entirely. The partsupp hash (256MB) remains as in iter_0 but gains a software prefetch for its hash slot 16 rows ahead to hide the remaining DRAM latency. CRITICAL: profit accumulation MUST use `double` (64-bit IEEE 754) — NOT `long double` (80-bit x87) — for all per-thread profit arrays and the global reduce. Using long double was the root cause of iter_3's regression. L3 hot budget: green_part 2MB + suppkey_to_nationrow 0.4MB + year_nibbles 28.6MB = 31MB, well within the 44MB LLC. No runtime structure building is performed.
